rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope to maintain architectural clarity
Preserve existing AADL structure and connections when inserting annex blocks
Use valid AGREE syntax and logical operators in contracts
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators such as and, or, not(...), and implication =>
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
